channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The 2025 Oscars won't use this 'lovely' format for the acting awards
item.description: The format was revived for the 2024 show — but it won't be repeated in the categories honoring the on-screen talent.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: USS Harry S. Truman ship collision damage revealed in new photos
item.description: New images released Saturday show the extent of the damage the USS Harry S. Truman suffered when it collided with a merchant vessel in the Mediterranean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What does 'Wawa' even mean? The company offers up a hint
item.description: In certain communities along the East Coast, the mere mention of the name “Wawa” conjures up visions of piping-hot coffee, scratch-off lottery tickets and pre-made Sizzli breakfast sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency’s plans, and cuts could come as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump moves with dizzying speed on his to-do list. But there are warning signs in his first month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump approaches the first-month mark in his second term, he has moved with dizzying speed and blunt force to reorder American social and political norms and the economy while redefining the U.S. role in the world.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, JOSH BOAK and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Missing man tortured and killed, 5 arrested: NY police
item.description: Five people have been arrested in connection to the death of Sam Nordquist — a Minnesota man who investigators said was tortured and killed.
item.dc:creator: George Gandy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Volunteers deliver gifts to widows on Valentine's Day. The project began in a florist's driveway
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jillian Myers knows the devastation of losing a husband and feeling alone and empty, particularly on Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rhode Island officers involved in shootout sue company for hearing loss
item.description: The lawsuit against the tactical company was filed three years to the day of the dramatic shootout on Denison street in Providence.
item.dc:creator: Tim White, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: A framed image of President Donald Trump's mug shot from when he was indicted in Georgia in 2023 is hanging outside the Oval Office in the latest bit of redecorating since he moved back in.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News embeds with ICE pursuing targets, including illegal migrant wanted for sexual assault conviction
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement's (ICE) Enforcement and Removal Operations in Houston gave Fox News an inside look at day-to-day operations, as officers make detentions and target illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cause of death for 'General Hospital' actress revealed
item.description: Former “General Hospital” actress Leslie Charleson’s death last month was the result of “sequelae of blunt head trauma,” according to the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner’s Office. Charleson, 79, had died on Jan. 12, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Deadly DC plane crash investigation examining helicopter altitude
item.description: In the weeks since the crash, the NTSB has been analyzing flight data and damage to determine how this happened.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Florida newlyweds get justice after family fishing trip ended in murder
item.description: A Florida killer found guilty of killing a newlywed couple in front of their toddler was finally executed on Thursday, nearly three decades after his gruesome crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: US Park Service erases the word 'transgender' from website commemorating Stonewall riot
item.description: References to transgender people were removed Thursday from a National Park Service website for the Stonewall National Monument, a park and visitor center in New York that commemorates a 1969 riot that became a pivotal moment for the LGBTQ+ rights movement.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alleged serial shoplifter banned from every Walmart in the USA
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A serial shoplifter has gotten herself banned from every Walmart in the United States of America due to her history of stealing from the company, Memphis Police say. Ashley Cross, 37, is charged with criminal trespass and theft of merchandise less than $1,000.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: Christian aid groups weigh life-threatening choices about who to help after USAID funding pause
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a warehouse in Haiti, nearly four metric tons of seeds cannot be distributed. Soon the planting season will be gone and with it, the best chance for those seeds to produce emergency food.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inflation ticks up to 3 percent annual increase in January
item.description: The consumer price index (CPI) increased by 0.5 percent in January to hit an annual increase of 3.0 percent after advancing by 2.9 percent in December.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: Jordan pledges to take in sick Palestinian children as Trump backpedals on aid threat
item.description: King Abdullah II of Jordan pledged to take in 2,000 sick Palestinian children from the Gaza Strip on Tuesday amid pressure from President Trump for his country to take in Palestinians from the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
